scenario: Nimbus payments runs 25 microservices in Camunda 8.8 and a single trigger fired from one orchestrator process must wake every process instance currently waiting at a corresponding catch event across multiple deployed processes in a one to many fan out, rather than targeting a single specific instance through correlation.
question: Which BPMN event type is appropriate for this single sender to many recipient collaboration?

options.text: A signal event whose broadcast triggers every catch event with a matching signal name across processes and forms a single sender to many recipient relationship by design
A message event whose publication targets one waiting process instance through correlation and forms a single sender to single recipient relationship between two collaborators
A signal event whose broadcast targets the oldest waiting process instance through correlation and forms a single sender to single recipient relationship between processes
A message event whose publication triggers every catch event with a matching message name across processes and forms a single sender to many recipient relationship across deployments

a.text: Correct. Signals are one to many collaboration: broadcasting a signal triggers every signal event with a matching name across processes, including every process instance currently waiting at a corresponding catch event.

b.text: Incorrect. Messages target a single process instance through correlation and form a one to one relationship; they cannot deliver the same event to multiple waiting instances at once.

c.text: Incorrect. Signals are not correlated to a single instance; broadcasting triggers all matching signal subscribers rather than picking the oldest waiting process instance.

d.text: Incorrect. Messages cannot have more than one recipient, so they form a one to one relationship; the multi recipient behaviour is the defining property of signals, not messages.

explanation: Signals model one to many collaboration: broadcasting iterates over subscriptions and triggers every signal event whose name matches, while message events are one to one because a message cannot have more than one recipient.
